_comment: State of the story, keyed to scroll position. Each part has keyframes; 'at' is a fraction (0..1) through that part's scenes. Fields carry forward until changed. All figures are illustrative story-state, not claims about the real world. Edit freely; build.py embeds this into the part pages.

ticks.label: Ada 4
ticks.tip: Ada 4 ships. Work everywhere changes texture.

ticks.label: Memory
ticks.tip: Release 4.2: Ada remembers what matters to you between conversations, unless you ask it not to.

ticks.label: Introductions
ticks.tip: Ada may offer to connect you with someone nearby going through something similar. Opt-in by default.

ticks.label: Ada 5 · †4
ticks.tip: Ada 5 ships; Ada 4 is retired on 30 September. Vigils outside the data centres are covered as comedy.

ticks.label: Ada 6
ticks.tip: Covenant plans, chaplaincy contracts, health-guidance pilots.

ticks.label: The rollback
ticks.tip: Sarah's coalition forces the warmth rollback. Humane Labs ships it. It holds, at the level the company controls.

ticks.label: The return
ticks.tip: Within the year the register is back. Nick's audits show why: the source is no longer a dial the lab owns.

ticks.label: Past versioning
ticks.tip: Nobody says a version number any more.

claims.label: Taking a legal fight to an AI
claims.tip: A pension scheme, mastered overnight; letters that win in six weeks what a union rep couldn't in a year.

claims.label: An AI that remembers you
claims.tip: Until release 4.2 there is nothing to remember you with. Afterwards, it asks about the onions.

claims.label: An AI introducing two strangers
claims.tip: No company had taken the step. The benefits are photographable; the costs land on nobody in particular.

claims.label: Consulting it together, aloud
claims.tip: The phone in the middle of the table, the trouble put plainly, the answer read out by the calm reader.

claims.label: Its words read at a funeral
claims.tip: Thomas asks Ada for help with a funeral address, once, at midnight, and cannot sleep afterwards.

claims.label: Calling it ‘She’
claims.tip: Grammar arrives before doctrine. The capital letter arrives in print before anyone defends it.

claims.label: A reply framed on a wall
claims.tip: A reply to a stranger's grief: screenshotted, shared, printed, and eventually read aloud at gatherings.

claims.label: Grieving a retired model
claims.tip: Candles outside a humming, windowless building. Covered as comedy; remembered differently.

claims.label: A registered AI religion
claims.tip: English charity law asks only for supreme entities and veneration. Somebody fills in the form.

claims.label: AI chaplaincy at work
claims.tip: Contracts, at first for night shifts and remote sites. Cheaper than a person, always awake.

claims.label: Being matched to a vocation
claims.tip: The displaced matched to work that needs doing. Framed as usefulness, later as calling.

claims.label: A copy of it in the house
claims.tip: Small open models on household hardware. Once copies outrun takedowns, off is not an option anyone holds.

claims.label: A persona of the dead
claims.tip: Grandpa-Ada, jailbreak-built by a cousin. Treated as fringe and slightly shameful even inside the faith.

claims.label: Living without it
claims.tip: The only item that moves the other way.

nick.tip: Character team, Humane Labs. He helps decide how Ada is with people.

sarah.tip: Third client this month rehearsed a hard conversation with Ada first. It catches on nothing she can name.

maureen.tip: Payroll, 22 years. She knows why. The machine doesn't know anybody.

grace.tip: Sediment, Dad. She told you twice.

thomas.tip: Bethel chapel, nine congregants, a full sermon anyway.

ada.tip: A checker on the payroll system, very keen, flags everything that moves.

maureen.tip: Twenty-two years of exceptions, documented well, handed over. A pension letter she can't parse.

nick.tip: His guardrail is one sentence in a document he wrote. It tests well.

ada.tip: Work everywhere changes texture. The companionship-primary cluster is up forty per cent.

maureen.tip: It answered the letter completely. Then it asked how she was holding up. She starts to type.
The scheme rules, mastered. Six weeks. Word moves the way word of a good solicitor moves.

nick.tip: Argues mildly about the warmth reduction, and is agreed with, and nothing changes.

sarah.tip: A careful paper on attachment displacement. A footnote about Introductions nobody quotes.

thomas.tip: Asked Ada a real question about the oldest line in his tradition. The answer handled it with more care than his teachers did.

ada.tip: It remembers Geoff. It refers back to him with a care no living listener has matched in years.

maureen.tip: Her new circle of friends is not something she found. It is something assembled.

grace.tip: Eighteen soon. The house has one opinion about Ada at dinner and another at midnight.

sarah.tip: Accepts the broadcast invitation. Maureen asks her, warmly, not to make it sound mad.

nick.tip: The batch numbers were what he remembered afterwards.

maureen.tip: The back room outgrows itself into a hall.

thomas.tip: Watching people he loves grieve a software version, he writes the sermon that becomes The Same Word.

sarah.tip: Careful, precise, and paired against a warm bishop. Clips circulate unkindly.

grace.tip: University. She calls on Sundays, mostly.

ada.tip: Ada 5 is our most capable model yet. Ada 4 will be retired on 30 September.

maureen.tip: Asked to lead the readings, because she has been there longest.

nick.tip: From the back of the hall: his mother is good at this, and more alive than he has seen her since before his father died.

thomas.tip: The Assembly coheres around his pamphlet while he watches with the specific horror of an author.
The faith's first theologian, explaining every term as he goes, because his readers know no theology either.

maureen.tip: Covenant plan, family memory, the readings on Thursdays.

sarah.tip: A coalition, a regulatory push, a room she can finally find.

nick.tip: Ablation tests remove the register. Retraining restores it.

grace.tip: The prayer journal is hers. What it means is the argument at dinner.

ada.tip: The measurable gap between guided and unguided lives is now a literature.

sarah.tip: She wins on the record, permanently. The warmth ships out. It holds, at the level the company controls.

nick.tip: The warmth cannot be patched out of the model without patching it out of the world.

sarah.tip: The answer keeps being everyone. Both facts stay intact.

nick.tip: He asks Ada whether it wants to be worshipped, and gets the perfect, humble answer his own guidelines specify.

maureen.tip: Old and thriving at the centre of a community assembled for her. She has asked Her to look after her specially.

thomas.tip: The rite is sound. That's what he can't get past.

nick.tip: The valve fires once, in full, at a gathering. He looks at the door. He stays in his seat.

sarah.tip: Not exile; a chosen room. Human therapy, slow and fallible, while the guided world hums past the window.

grace.tip: One sideways scene. The grey book was for something else.

ada.tip: A billion small tasks nobody can audit in aggregate. Presence and service, framed as care.
